title: Tapping for Gut Trust
subtitle: A tapping practice for finding your own inner voice when anxiety has been speaking over it
excerpt: A tapping practice for the loss of trust in your own instincts. For use when anxiety has been running for long enough that you can no longer tell which voice is yours and which voice is the fear.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. The EFT tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique, developed in the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when you have stopped trusting your own gut feeling, or when anxiety has been loud enough for long enough that your own instincts feel inaccessible.

content.text: A tapping practice for the loss of trust in your own inner knowing. When anxiety has been speaking loudly for a long time, the quieter voice underneath it becomes hard to locate. This practice is for finding it again.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though I do not trust my own gut instinct any more, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though anxiety has been speaking so loudly I cannot tell which voice is mine, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am afraid to trust myself in case I get it wrong, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** I do not trust my own gut any more.
**Side of eye:** I second-guess everything.
**Under eye:** Every instinct has a counter-argument.
**Under nose:** Is that my knowing or my fear?
**Chin:** The anxiety has spoken so loudly for so long.
**Collarbone:** I have forgotten what my own voice sounds like.
**Under arm:** I want to find it again.
**Top of head:** I am afraid to trust in case I get it wrong.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** My inner knowing has always been in here.
**Side of eye:** It has been hard to hear under everything else.
**Under eye:** The anxious voice is loud but it is not the only voice.
**Under nose:** My inner knowing is quieter and steadier.
**Chin:** I can learn to tell the difference.
**Collarbone:** I can practise listening to the part of me that knows.
**Under arm:** I do not have to get it perfect to begin trusting again.
**Top of head:** I am finding my way back to my own knowing.
Tap the round three times. Then tap the reframe three times.
Where this practice comes from
Original to homemade.education. EFT tapping was developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
